title: After Action Review — Talent Generation Balance
scope: Rebalance the talent generation system to eliminate the glut of 90+ OVR players that was causing unrealistic 100+ point games and compressing the competitive landscape.

rationale.item: Blue blood rosters were wall-to-wall 93-99 OVR with stat_center=95 and stat_spread=3 — razor-tight 92-98 band; players had no profile
Transfer portal seniors generated up to 93 OVR base with +2-6 position boosts, routinely producing 95+ players from thin air
Stat spreads universally too narrow (3-6) — every player on a team felt like a clone; no real depth chart drama
No ceiling or budget constraints — gaussian pile-up at 99 ceiling; no mechanism preventing a player from being elite at everything

actions.description: Archetype stat centers dropped ~30 points (blue_blood: 95→64, national_power: 89→52, etc.); spreads unified to 18; per-stat ceiling lowered to 96; stat budget system added
Recruiting stat ranges shifted down 30 (5-star: 83-98→49-63, etc.); position boost caps lowered to 99; OVR floor 40→10
Transfer portal ranges shifted down 30; 5-star potential 10%→6%; position boost caps lowered to 96
Conference floors adjusted proportionally (~30 points lower)

risk_posture: Possible over-correction on scoring — if games become too low-scoring, centers can be nudged up 3-5 points without touching the spread/ceiling/budget infrastructure. The 30-point drop is aggressive.
Multiplier dependency: this change assumes the in-game multiplier system is robust enough to differentiate teams with similar raw stats.
UI/display consideration: Player cards will now show stats in the 30s-70s range — may initially feel 'wrong' to players accustomed to seeing high numbers.
residual_risks: Existing dynasty saves unaffected — inflated talent retained until natural attrition cycles players out
Conference floor interaction may need tuning if SEC doormats (floor 25) are too competitive with Big East powers
In-game multiplier system must be robust enough to differentiate teams starting with similar raw stats
open_hooks: Tuning levers documented: stat_center (generate_rosters.py), stat_spread (38), generation ceiling (85), stat budget headroom, development gains (development.py), development ceiling (99), hidden gem boost, portal ranges, recruit ranges
section_headers_original: After Action Review — Talent Generation Balance
1. Stat centers were calibrated for ceiling, not for raw talent
2. Gaussian spreads were too narrow to create roster texture
3. Three independent talent pipelines all skewed high
5. Hidden gems compounded the problem at the top
1. Stat centers dropped ~30 points across all tiers (scripts/generate_rosters.py)
2. Spreads unified to 18 across all tiers
unmapped_content: Branch: claude/balance-talent-generation-mS413. Archetype table before/after: doormat 28→15, underdog 50→20, punching_above 65→35, regional_power 78→44, national_power 89→52, blue_blood 95→64. Spread unified to 18 (was 3-6). Base OVR ranges after: doormat ~10-27, blue_blood ~52-76. Stat budget: center×11+44. Portal ranges: soph/jr 65-90→28-52, senior 70-93→32-56. Conference floors: SEC 60→25, Big Ten 55→20, ACC 69→32. Note: this document covers the same topic as docs/feature_report_talent_generation_rebalance_2026_03_27.md which was dated 2026-03-27 and includes an additional hidden runtime bug (team_center_offset) not mentioned here. These may be AAR + follow-up report of the same workstream.
parser_notes: Date confirmed: 2026-03-25. No validation claims or non_actions. Related document: docs/feature_report_talent_generation_rebalance_2026_03_27.md covers the same workstream with more detail (additional team_center_offset bug, additional files including game_engine.py and development.py) — dated 2026-03-27.
